questions.text: The area under a velocity-time graph represents:
questions.explanation: The integral of velocity over time gives the change in position (displacement).

questions.text: A particle moves with constant acceleration 'a'. If the initial velocity is 'u', the distance traveled in 't' seconds is:
questions.explanation: This is the second equation of motion for constant acceleration.

questions.text: Which of the following is a vector quantity?
questions.explanation: Displacement has both magnitude and direction, unlike speed or distance.

questions.text: The slope of a displacement-time graph at any instant gives:
questions.explanation: The derivative dx/dt represents instantaneous velocity.

questions.text: If a body is thrown vertically upwards, its velocity at the highest point is:
questions.explanation: At the peak, the body momentarily stops before changing direction.

questions.text: The ratio of displacement to distance is always:
questions.explanation: Displacement is the shortest path; distance is the actual path. Displacement ≤ Distance.

questions.text: A car covers half of the distance at speed v1 and the other half at v2. The average speed is:
questions.explanation: Harmonic mean is used for average speed when distances are equal.

questions.text: Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of:
questions.explanation: Acceleration a = dv/dt.

questions.text: If the velocity-time graph is a straight line parallel to the time axis, the acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Parallel to the time axis means velocity is constant, so acceleration is zero.

questions.text: A body starts from rest and moves with a constant acceleration of 2 m/s². The distance covered in 5 seconds is:
questions.explanation: s = ut + ½at² = 0 + ½(2)(5)² = 25 m.

questions.text: Negative acceleration is also called:
questions.explanation: Retardation or deceleration refers to acceleration in the direction opposite to velocity.

questions.text: The distance traveled by a particle is proportional to the square of time. Its acceleration is:
questions.explanation: s ∝ t² => s = kt². v = ds/dt = 2kt. a = dv/dt = 2k (constant).

questions.text: A ball is dropped from a height. If it takes 't' seconds to reach the ground, the height is:
questions.explanation: Using h = ut + ½gt² with u=0.

questions.text: Which graph represents uniform motion?
options: Straight line through origin in s-t graph
Straight line parallel to s-axis in s-t graph
questions.explanation: In uniform motion, displacement increases linearly with time.

questions.text: Relative velocity of A with respect to B (v_AB) is:
questions.explanation: v_AB = v_A - v_B by definition.

questions.text: Two trains are moving in opposite directions with speeds v1 and v2. Their relative speed is:
The slope of the velocity-time graph gives:
questions.explanation: a = dv/dt, which is the slope of the v-t graph.

questions.text: Can a body have zero velocity and non-zero acceleration?
options: Yes, at the start of motion
Yes, at the highest point of vertical throw
questions.explanation: At the highest point, v=0 but a=g. At start, v=0 but a can be non-zero.

questions.text: Which of the following cannot be negative?
questions.explanation: Distance is the magnitude of path length and is always scalar and non-negative.

questions.text: A body is moving with constant velocity. Its acceleration-time graph is:
options: A line coinciding with the time axis
A line parallel to acceleration axis
questions.explanation: Constant velocity means zero acceleration (a=0).

questions.text: The displacement of a body is given by s = 3t² + 2t + 5. The velocity at t=2s is:
If a body covers unequal distances in equal intervals of time, the motion is:
questions.explanation: This is the definition of non-uniform motion.

questions.text: For a stationary object, the position-time graph is:
questions.explanation: Position does not change with time.

questions.text: The kinematics equation v² - u² = 2as is derived assuming acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Standard equations of motion are valid only for constant acceleration.

questions.text: Average velocity is defined as:
questions.explanation: Average velocity is a vector, calculated using displacement.

questions.text: When a body is moving with constant acceleration, the velocity-time graph is:
questions.explanation: v = u + at is a linear equation (y = mx + c).

questions.text: An object travels 10m north and then 10m east. Its displacement is:
questions.explanation: Displacement = sqrt(10² + 10²) = 10*sqrt(2) ≈ 14.14m.

questions.text: Instantaneous speed is:
options: Always equal to magnitude of instantaneous velocity
questions.explanation: At any instant, speed is the magnitude of the velocity vector.

questions.text: If a body is moving with decreasing velocity, its acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Decrease in velocity implies negative acceleration (retardation).

questions.text: The path length of a moving body can never be:
questions.explanation: Distance (path length) is a scalar quantity that always accumulates and cannot be negative.

questions.text: A jet plane moving at 500 km/h ejects its products of combustion at a speed of 1500 km/h relative to the jet. The speed of the products relative to the ground is:
In a non-uniform acceleration motion, the velocity-time graph is:
questions.explanation: Non-constant rate of change of velocity results in a curved graph.

questions.text: The motion of a train on a straight track is an example of:
questions.explanation: Motion along a straight line is rectilinear motion.

questions.text: Acceleration due to gravity near Earth's surface is approximately:
questions.explanation: The standard value of 'g' used in physics problems.

questions.text: Which kinematic equation does not involve displacement?
questions.explanation: v = u + at relates velocity, acceleration, and time.

questions.text: If a ball is thrown up with velocity u, the time it takes to return to the hand is:
questions.explanation: Time to peak is u/g, total time is 2 * u/g.

questions.text: A particle moves on a circular path of radius R. The displacement after half a circle is:
questions.explanation: Displacement is the diameter (2R). Distance would be πR.

questions.text: One-dimensional motion is also called:
questions.explanation: Motion in a single straight line.

questions.text: A body is said to be in motion if its position changes with:
questions.explanation: Motion is the change of position over time relative to a frame of reference.

questions.text: The unit of acceleration in SI is:
questions.explanation: Meters per second squared.
